title: QK norm
description: Query-key normalization that rescales attention queries and keys before their dot products, changing the attention score path rather than the whole residual stream.
openingSummary: QK norm stabilizes attention by normalizing the query and key vectors right before their dot products, which narrows the change to the score path instead of renormalizing the entire hidden stream around every transformer sublayer.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: QK norm applies normalization directly to the query and key vectors used in attention scoring. Instead of normalizing the whole residual stream, it targets the part of the block that decides how sharply one token attends to another.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: QK norm mainly targets attention-logit stability. It reduces the chance that raw query or key magnitude will make the softmax too sharp, especially in deeper models or longer-context settings.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: The block projects hidden states into queries and keys, normalizes those vectors, then forms attention scores from the normalized versions before the usual softmax and value mixing. The attention pattern depends more on direction and less on raw vector length.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: The formula below shows the attention score path after normalized queries and keys are substituted into the standard dot product. The hats mark the normalized forms.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Layer norm and RMSNorm usually normalize the hidden state that flows through a whole transformer block. QK norm is narrower: it only changes the query-key score path inside attention. That makes it more of a targeted attention tweak than a general residual-stream norm.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: QK norm appears in some transformer variants that want extra control over attention sharpness, especially when long context or deep stacks make logits harder to keep in a comfortable range.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: QK norm only solves one narrow problem. It does not replace the broader stability role of layer norm or RMSNorm across the residual stream, so many architectures still need a separate hidden-state normalization strategy.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: QK norm is a good reminder that normalization is not only about whole-layer placement. Sometimes the most useful intervention is inside one sensitive operation, such as attention scoring.

computeFlow.alt: QK norm flow from query and key projections to normalized scores and attended output

qk.placement: Inside attention before the dot product
qk.mainBenefit: Keeps attention logits from becoming too sharp

rms.mainBenefit: Stabilizes scale with less work than layer norm

v.definition: Value vectors used after the attention weights are formed.

dk.definition: Key dimension used in the score scaling factor.
